title: Set-StrictMode -Version Latest bleeds into parent event handlers during ShowDialog
description: When a child script called via & sets Set-StrictMode -Version Latest, then calls $form.ShowDialog(), the WinForms message pump dispatches parent-scope event handlers (timer ticks, FormClosing, Resize) within the child scripts strict-mode context. Any $script:-scoped variable in those handlers that is not set in the child scripts scope throws 'The variable cannot be retrieved because it has not been set.' This causes: (1) Timer tick handlers to error on every tick, (2) FormClosing handlers to crash, preventing graceful close/minimize-to-tray, (3) Child form ShowDialog to throw, breaking the child tool entirely.
ps_version_scope_note: WinForms ShowDialog StrictMode scope bleed is a PS5.1 Desktop edition concern. In PS7 Core the WinForms message pump behaves the same but code should be audited per deployment target.
detection_method: Scan for scripts that contain both Set-StrictMode -Version Latest and .ShowDialog() calls. Cross-reference with parent scripts that have event handlers referencing $script: variables.
known_instances: Invoke-PSEnvironmentScanner.ps1: ShowDialog caused $script:_ForceClose and $script:_ServiceTimer errors in Main-GUI.ps1 event handlers
exception_message: The variable '$script:_ForceClose' cannot be retrieved because it has not been set.
preventionRule:
1. Before calling ShowDialog() in any child script that uses Set-StrictMode -Version Latest, temporarily relax strict mode: Set-StrictMode -Off before ShowDialog, restore after.
2. In parent event handlers (timer ticks, FormClosing), wrap $script: variable access in try/catch with sensible defaults: $val = try { $script:_MyVar } catch { $defaultValue }.
3. Never assume $script: variables from a parent script will be accessible under strict mode in WinForms event handlers fired during a child scripts ShowDialog().
trusted_fix_notes: Applied to Invoke-PSEnvironmentScanner.ps1 (StrictMode toggle) and Main-GUI.ps1 (null-guard in timer tick + try/catch in FormClosing).

attempts.action: Added StrictMode toggle in scanner, null-guards in Main-GUI handlers
